Uses of Interface
com.github.cowwoc.requirements.java.StringVerifier

Packages that use StringVerifier
Package
Description
com.github.cowwoc.requirements
The Requirements, DefaultRequirements endpoints.
com.github.cowwoc.requirements.java
Classes used to verify preconditions, postconditions and invariants of core Java types.
com.github.cowwoc.requirements.java.extension
Interfaces that may be extended but not implemented (because the methods contain a generic return type).
com.github.cowwoc.requirements.java.internal
Design guide
com.github.cowwoc.requirements.java.internal.extension

    Method
    Description
    static StringVerifier

    Verifies the requirements of a String.
    StringVerifier

    Methods in com.github.cowwoc.requirements.java that return StringVerifier
    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    StringVerifier
    StringVerifier.asBigDecimal(Consumer<BigDecimalVerifier> consumer)
    Validates nested requirements.
    StringVerifier
    StringVerifier.asBoolean(Consumer<BooleanVerifier> consumer)
    Verifies nested requirements.
    StringVerifier
    StringVerifier.asInetAddress(Consumer<InetAddressVerifier> consumer)
    Verifies nested requirements.
    StringVerifier
    StringVerifier.asInteger(Consumer<IntegerVerifier<Integer>> consumer)
    Validates nested requirements.
    StringVerifier
    StringVerifier.asLong(Consumer<IntegerVerifier<Long>> consumer)
    Validates nested requirements.
    StringVerifier
    StringVerifier.asShort(Consumer<IntegerVerifier<Short>> consumer)
    Validates nested requirements.
    StringVerifier
    StringVerifier.asString()
    Deprecated.
    actual is already a String
    StringVerifier
    StringVerifier.asUri(Consumer<UriVerifier> consumer)
    Verifies nested requirements.
    StringVerifier
    StringVerifier.asUrl(Consumer<UrlVerifier> consumer)
    Verifies nested requirements.
    StringVerifier
    StringVerifier.contains(String expected)
    Ensures that the actual value contains a value.
    StringVerifier
    StringVerifier.doesNotContain(String value)
    Ensures that the actual value does not contain a value.
    StringVerifier
    StringVerifier.doesNotEndWith(String suffix)
    Ensures that the actual value does not end with a value.
    StringVerifier
    StringVerifier.doesNotStartWith(String prefix)
    Ensures that the actual value does not start with a value.
    StringVerifier
    StringVerifier.endsWith(String suffix)
    Ensures that the actual value ends with a value.
    StringVerifier
    StringVerifier.isBlank()
    Ensures that the actual value is empty or contains only white space codepoints.
    StringVerifier
    StringVerifier.isEmpty()
    Ensures that the actual value is empty.
    StringVerifier
    StringVerifier.isNotBlank()
    Ensures that the actual value is not empty or contains non-white space codepoints.
    StringVerifier
    StringVerifier.isNotEmpty()
    Ensures that the actual value is not empty.
    StringVerifier
    StringVerifier.isStripped()
    Ensures that the actual value does not contain leading or trailing white space, where white space is defined by strip().
    StringVerifier
    StringVerifier.isTrimmed()
    Ensures that the actual value does not contain leading or trailing white space, where white space is defined by trim().
    StringVerifier
    StringVerifier.length(Consumer<SizeVerifier> consumer)
    Verifies nested requirements.
    StringVerifier
    DefaultJavaRequirements.requireThat(String actual, String name)
     
    StringVerifier
    JavaRequirements.requireThat(String actual, String name)
    Verifies the requirements of a String.
    StringVerifier
    StringVerifier.startsWith(String prefix)
    Ensures that the actual value starts with a value.
    StringVerifier
    StringVerifier.strip()
    Strips white space from the beginning and the end of the actual value, where white space is defined by the Unicode standard.
    StringVerifier
    StringVerifier.trim()
    Removes all white space from the beginning and end of the actual value, where white space is defined as any character whose codepoint is less than or equal to 'U+0020' (the space character).
